content: Laravel 11 seeding: firstOrCreate(key, attrs) makes seeders idempotent by matching on the key array and only creating if absent, returning the existing row on rerun. For pivot tables, seed via the relationship's sync()/attach() inside the parent creation, or loop firstOrCreate on the pivot model's unique key pair. DatabaseSeeder::run() should call sub-seeders in dependency order via $this->call([...]) — parent tables (users/classrooms/subjects) before child tables (exams/questions/attempts) that reference them.
